overview.title: Spec-Driven Development Pipeline
overview.description: Convert arcforge's one-shot brainstorming-refiner-planner pipeline into an iterable Spec-Driven Development workflow. Design docs become immutable raw sources; specs become live contracts updated via delta integration; DAGs become derived views that preserve execution state. Three foundational rules (R1 Human Consent, R2 Unidirectional Flow, R3 Block on Contradictions) govern the entire flow.

includes.feature: Scan existing specs, confirm spec target, produce design doc with context-sensitive sections (prose when no prior spec; Context + Change Intent when prior spec exists), Phase 0 pending-conflict detection with iterate-branch auto-entry, structured decision-log Q&A output, output validation

One behavior with conditional fields based on filesystem state, wiki-style accumulating delta output, DAG completion gate upstream of iteration, three-axis R3 contradiction check (design internal, design ↔ Q&A, criterion coverage), Phase 5 no-invention discipline, Phase 5.5 self-contradiction sub-pass, R3 block via terminal (no authoritative artifact; ephemeral _pending-conflict.md handoff to brainstorming on axis-1/2/3 block), input/output validation including Phase 6 mechanical authorization check over <trace> elements

Pure function (spec + delta) → (dag.yaml + epics/), overwrite DAG each sprint (no archive, no state preservation, no gate), delta-scoped planning with epic generation for added/modified/removed/renamed, 1:1 mapping, done signal, input/output validation
R1 (human consent), R2 (unidirectional), R3 (block on contradictions)
Directory structure for designs, specs, DAG (per-spec), and the ephemeral _pending-conflict.md handoff
XML schema for spec self-identification, versioning, and wiki-style accumulating delta metadata
Design Doc, Spec Identity Header, _pending-conflict.md handoff, and structured decision-log format contracts — sdd-schemas/ (guidance) + sdd-utils.js (validation toolkit)

Input/output validation at each pipeline stage with three-tier severity, two-pass write, deterministic/semantic split, and Phase 6 mechanical authorization check (axis 3 enforcement at validator level)

excludes.reason: Cross-spec dependencies — deferred until multi-spec usage is established
Downstream migration (Phase 2) — coordinator.js per-spec DAG support, CLI --spec parameter, loop.js multi-spec parallelism, skill path updates, worktree marker spec_id

Stage 3 cross-model audit pass — proposal's optional defense-in-depth (different-model judge re-reads in-memory spec inside Phase 5.5); deferred unless same-model-bias check shows >1 finding divergence

Cleanup of deprecated fr-cc-if-003 (SDD Schemas Guidance Layer) — the v1 requirement that scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/design.md and spec.md exist remains valid as artifact-location spec; per fr-sd-011-ac3 those files are deprecated as SoT but remain as artifacts. Removing fr-cc-if-003 would also require removing those files; that scope-creep does not belong in this R3-enforcement iteration.

constraint.title: Zero External Dependencies
constraint.description: Node.js standard library only. No npm runtime dependencies.

constraint.title: Three-Tier Validation Severity
constraint.description: All per-stage validation uses three severity levels: ERROR (blocks stage, no output produced), WARNING (continues but alerts user), INFO (informational). A stage is "valid" when it has zero ERRORs.

constraint.title: Self-Contained Stage Input
constraint.description: Each pipeline stage receives a self-contained input: the complete design doc for refiner, the complete spec.xml for planner. Stages MUST NOT rely on implicit context from prior conversation or session state.
